content: iframe srcdoc + sandbox attribute is the standard technique for live-previewing user-generated HTML/CSS/JS. Critical: sandbox with 'allow-scripts' alone keeps the iframe cross-origin from the parent (parent cannot read/interact with iframe content), which is what you want for isolation. Adding 'allow-same-origin' together with 'allow-scripts' defeats the sandbox (iframe can then reach the parent DOM) and must be avoided unless strictly necessary. Known escape vectors: target=_blank links with javascript: hrefs and window.opener can break out even when sandboxed — set sandbox without allow-popups and avoid allow-top-navigation. Since this project generates srcdoc from data the app itself constructs from constrained drag-drop/rule-builder inputs (not raw free-text), injection risk is lower than a general code playground, but sandbox='allow-scripts' (no allow-same-origin, no allow-popups) is still the correct default.
